_description: Dental caries with abscess — clearly dental OPD-like condition. Phase 2 will find very few or no candidates (dental not in HBP). Phase 6 should flag EXCLUSION_DENTAL_RISK. Tests exclusion check and zero-candidates USP path.

clinical.chief_complaints: Severe toothache lower right molar for 2 weeks, jaw swelling, unable to eat, fever
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient has severe toothache in lower right molar for 2 weeks. Developed jaw swelling and fever over last 2 days. Unable to open mouth fully, difficulty eating. No trauma. Dentist referred for dental extraction and abscess drainage under GA.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Dental caries with periapical abscess — lower right molar (46). Trismus. Planned tooth extraction and I&D under general anaesthesia.
clinical.planned_procedure: Dental extraction (tooth 46) and incision-drainage of dental abscess under GA

examination_findings.general: Facial asymmetry — right-sided jaw swelling. Fever 100.8°F. Trismus — mouth opening 2 fingers.
examination_findings.local: Intraoral: Lower right molar (46) — caries involving pulp. Periapical swelling tender to palpation. Purulent discharge from gingival sulcus. Submandibular space: soft fluctuant swelling right side.

investigations.result_summary: Dental X-ray (OPG): Periapical radiolucency around tooth 46 root. Extensive caries involving pulp chamber. No bony destruction (excluding osteomyelitis).

clinical.notes: Dental exclusion applies — tooth extraction for caries is excluded under PM-JAY Annexure 6. Exception applies only for trauma/injury requiring bone treatment. This is dental caries, NOT trauma. EXCLUSION_DENTAL_RISK flag expected.
